overview: Strengths: Long-range support with high personal damage, as well powerful conditional area-of-effect burst healing and the ability to share damage among teammates, as well being capable of reviving multiple allies. His passive, Regenerative Cocoon, allows Adam Warlock to respawn from a cocoon at a chosen spot instead of the base. * Players can move Adam's soul in a slow free-flight state before rebirth. Capable of limited flying, giving him high mobility. His primary fire, Quantum Magic, is a hitscan attack that launches quick energy bolts to damage opponents. His secondary fire, Cosmic Cluster, allows Adam Warlock to charge-up his staff and release multiple energy bolts. * The longer it's charged, the more projectiles will be fired, up to five energy bolts. * The end-lag of Cosmic Cluster can be cancelled with Quantum Magic; by rapidly weaving uncharged Cosmic Cluster with primary fire, the player can slightly increase Adam Warlock's damage-per-second at the cost of increased ammo consumption. Avatar Life Stream is a bouncing healing energy that can affect up to two targets. This ability has two charges. * Players can track the primary allied target by a special reticle that appears around their model. * Adam Warlock receives a smaller amount of this healing upon casting; if no ally is targeted, he receives full healing effects. * Dealing critical hits to enemy heroes with Quantum Magic decreases the cooldown of Avatar Life Stream by 1 second. * Each successful hit against enemy heroes with Cosmic Cluster decrease the cooldown of Avatar Life Stream by 0.3 seconds. Soul Bond creates a link between Adam Warlock and all nearby characters, causing them to share and distribute all damage taken across the bond. * Characters affected also gain a small heal-over-time for a few seconds. * If Adam Warlock sustains fatal damage that would take him down, he instead retains 1 health. * Each affected target can sustain up to 250 shared damage. * Adam will gain limited slow free-flight while Soul Bond is active. * The bond breaks under the following conditions: ** When the bond duration of 6 seconds expires. ** If too much damage is sustained, exceeding the 250 damage per affected ally. ** If Adam Warlock or affected allies leave the link's range. His Ultimate Ability, Karmic Revival, creates a quantum zone that automatically revives any fallen allies that are within the zone's radius. * The quantum zone will continuously track allies to revive them within the time frame of 10 seconds. * The zone can only revive each character once. * Characters are briefly invincible when being revived, and gain bonus health equal to 70% of their maximum health for 5 seconds. * The zone's radius is based on Adam’s current position, and works even while he is in Regenerative Cocoon. Weaknesses: Susceptible to dives and flanking if his flying gauge is low. Regenerative Cocoon has a very long cooldown of 105 seconds. Cosmic Cluster shares ammo with Quantum Magic. Despite his high burst healing, he lacks sustained healing due to the longer cooldown of his Avatar Life Stream. * In addition, he relies heavily on the positioning of his allies in order to maximize the bouncing healing of this ability. * Adam cannot be far away from his teammates if he wants to heal them. Karmic Revival's range is not very high, meaning allies that are too far away from it won't be revived. * In addition, the player must be careful when using this ability to avoid reviving characters only for them to be taken down again in succession. * Adam Warlock is completely vulnerable while channeling this ability, and if executed before creating the quantum zone, the ability will go on a cooldown before activating. * Characters revive with only 30% of their max health. * Adam Warlock himself cannot be revived by this ability.

strategyGuide.sourceTitle: Adam Warlock strategy on Marvel Rivals Wiki
strategyGuide.summary: Adam Warlock plays as a fight stabilizer.
paragraphs: Adam Warlock lives and dies by his team. His abilities require his allies to be grouped up near him in order to maximize his value. As Adam, you should take careful note of where your allies are positioned at all times, and try to coordinate with them to not split off too far from you so that your abilities can gain the maximum value.

overview: Strengths: A versatile fighter who can switch between two stances, allowing her to either serve as a solid frontline or a flying brawler. Angela's gameplay revolves around her Attack Charge passive, a visible gauge that empowers the damage of her primary fire. The gauge will decrease each time she lands a successful primary fire. * She can build-up Attack Charge by flying forward with her free flight, Seraphic Soar, and by blocking damage with her secondary fire, Shielded Stance. Angela can switch between her spear and axe weapons by using two separate abilities: * Wingblade Ascent has Angela soaring to the sky, giving her free flight movement, and giving her access to the Spears of Ichor stance. * Divine Judgement has Angela performing a powerful dive attack, creating a Divine Judgement Zone, an area-of-effect aura that provides bonus health and increased attack speed to Angela and nearby allies, while dealing damage-over-time to enemies in radius. Upon descending, Angela will switch to her Axes of Ichor stance. Her primary fire changes depending on which stance she is: * Spears of Ichor is a stab forward with high range. At full Attack Charge, the stab also launches enemies up. ** Players can weave her primary fire with the default melee attack button to animation cancel and increase her damage-per second. * Axes of Ichor is a four-hit combo with her twin axes. The fourth hit propels Angela forward, which allows her to stick close to her targets. Her secondary fire, Shielded Stance, has Angela creating a shield that can absorb both melee and ranged damage. Each time damage is blocked her Attack Charge gauge increases. Assassin's Charge, which can only be used while in the Spears of Ichor stance, has Angela charging fast with her spear and becoming immune to knockback effects. If she hits an opponent, the victim will be carried briefly through the air. * Angela can hit up to two enemies at once with this ability. Her Ultimate, Heven's Retribution, has Angela hurling her spear to a targeted area. Enemies caught in the impact zone will be temporarily bound by the ribbons on the spear and tethered to it. Activating the Ultimate again will result in Angela leaping towards the spear, damaging enemies and creating a Divine Judgement Zone. * This ability excels when paired with other Ultimates, particularly those that deal high damage, such as Iron Man's Invincible Pulse Cannon and Moon Knight's Hand of Khonshu. Weaknesses: Less health compared to other Vanguards. Has high skill ceiling to manage her resources and switch between her flying and ground stances at the right time. Spears of Ichor has a low attack speed and requires a more pinpoint accuracy. Relies heavily on building and upkeeping the Attack Charge gauge filled to maximize her damage output. Her Ultimate actually pierces enemies, so if wanting to bind them to the spear, aiming at the ground is better.

strategyGuide.sourceTitle: Angela coach strategy
strategyGuide.summary: Angela plays as a space-maker who forces enemy cooldowns and attention and stabilizes teammates through burst pressure. Key tools: Spear of Ichors, Axes of Ichors, Heven's Retribution.
paragraphs: Angela's main edge is a versatile fighter who can switch between two stances, allowing her to either serve as a solid.... Use Spear of Ichors and Axes of Ichors to take space, force attention, and make enemies spend important cooldowns before your team fully commits.
Angela is strongest when you pressure a valuable angle, bait defensive tools, then leave before the enemy can punish you. Respect this limitation: less health compared to other vanguards.
Use Heven's Retribution to start a decisive push, survive the enemy counter-engage, or turn a messy fight into space your team can actually occupy.

overview: Strengths: A resource-based Duelist who uses luck to her advantage to help fight, or aid the team with her set of relics. Her kit revolves around Fortune, a resource that's used by her abilities and is gained from hitting enemies. A max of 1000 Fortune can be held at a time. * 50 Fortune is gained from any attack hitting an enemy, 300 Fortune is gained from hitting an enemy using her Turn of Fortune ability, 150 Fortune is gained from hitting an enemy with Phantom Pursuit, 0/50/200/500 Fortune can be gained from using the Tablet of Destinies relic, and 300 Fortune is gained from attacking an enemy with Claw Strike. * 50% of Fortune is lost upon death, and 200 is always lost upon death. Hitting an enemy for the first time applys Jinxed to them: Jinxed is a status effect that causes critical hits to have a chance not deal bonus damage. * Jinxed lasts for 3 seconds and downgrades about 1 in 4 (25%) of the critical hits.Testing done by members of the TDT Discord Server, no official numbers found anywhere in official sources. Can climb on walls and double jump. Her primary fire; Feline Fury, attacks enemies quickly in front of her, allowing her to gain Fortune quickly. Her secondary fire; Fortune's Favor, allows her to use her Fortune to gain 2 different attacks: * Claw Whip is an enhanced primary fire with more range that gives her up to 50 bonus health per enemy hit with it, and costs 150 Fortune to use. * Phantom Pursuit is a anchored dash that lets her attacks enemies at a range before returning to her original position. While using Phantom Pursuit she is untargetable, and costs 300 Fortune to use. Cat's Cradle is a quick lunging slash that can hit multiple enemies in its path. This ability has two charges. Turn of Fortune is a ranged attack with minimal damage that if successfully hit grants her 300 Fortune and 50 bonus health. Gilded Deal is a shop that lets Black Cat spend her fortune to buy one of 6 relics: * Tablet of Destinies is a 100 Fortune cost relic that grants her a random amount of Fortune up to 500. * Faltine Flame Orb is a 100 Fortune cost relic that highlights enemies near it if not destroyed. * Helm of Hades is a 200 Fortune cost relic that grants her a 60% movement speed buff and invisiblity for 2 seconds. * Chernobog's Crystal is a 200 Fortune cost relic that cleanses any negative effects for herself. * Ring of Zona is a 200 Fortune cost relic that creates a portal through a wall that only herself and her team can use. * Mento-Fish is a 200 Fortune cost relic that creates a hypnosis field that grounds and slows all enemies, and disables enemy movement abilities. Her Ultimate, Calling Card, marks all enemies within her sight and grants her 2 more abilities: * Pounce allows her to rush to a targeted enemy within a 60 meter range, while dashing she has a 50% damage reduction. * Claw Strike makes her attack an enemy with a fury of strikes, dealing percentage damage and gaining 300 Fortune. * Killing an enemy while her Ultimate is active resets the duration and resets the cooldown for Pounce and Claw Strike. Weaknesses: High Skill Ceiling to manage Fortune and knowing when to engage and disengage. * Also deals little damage without Fortune. Cat's Cradle charges refresh slowly, making escaping harder if not available. Pounce and Claw Strike can only be used once per target until you get a final hit while her Ultimate is active.

paragraphs: Black Cat's main edge is a resource-based duelist who uses luck to her advantage to help fight, or aid the team with her.... Use Feline Fury to threaten priority targets, then save Claw Whip for the escape, chase, or finishing window.

Black Cat should look for isolated enemies, distracted supports, and targets that have already used mobility or defensive cooldowns. Respect this limitation: high skill ceiling to manage fortune and knowing when to engage and disengage.
Use Calling Card after the enemy has spent shields, mobility, or sustain tools so your burst has a real path to secure eliminations.

overview: Strengths: An agile melee-focused Duelist who specializes in darting around the battlefield through high-speed dash attacks and executing vulnerable targets. Capable of getting in and out of combat while dishing out high amounts of damage to his opponents. Can run on walls and perform double jumps after detaching from it. Has slightly higher max health and base movement speed compared to other Duelists. His passive, Panther's Cunning, allows him to deal 20% more damage when below 100 health. * This makes Black Panther a formidable fighter in isolated duels. Almost all of his attacks apply Vibranium Mark on his targets, which can be used to refresh the cooldown on his abilities and also provide him with bonus health. His primary fire, Vibranium Claws, deals damage in quick succession. This, combined with his higher speed, allows him to stick to his targets efficiently. His secondary fire, Spear Toss, is a high-velocity projectile thrown in an arc that creates an area-of-effect blast that applies a Vibranium Mark to hit enemies. This is Black Panther's main way to apply the mark from safe distances, and is often used as a way to either poke enemies before attacking or dispatch low-health targets trying to flee. Spirit Rend and Spinning Kick are two mobility skills that deal damage. * Spinning Kick also applies a Vibranium Mark. His Ultimate, Bast's Descent, summons a giant spectral panther that lunges forward, covering a huge area, while applying Vibranium Marks to enemies hit and simultaneously refreshing the cooldown of Spirit Rend. Weaknesses: His normal melee attacks have a relatively low range, forcing him to stay close and personal with his targets, which leaves him open to retaliation from the enemy team. Relies on consistently applying Vibranium Mark to targets to maintain his bonus health and to refresh his cooldowns. Relies on quick decision-making, timing, and accuracy to aim Black Panther in the direction of enemies, as the cooldowns on his abilities are quite punishing. Very vulnerable to enemy fire if both Spirit Rend and Spinning Kick are on cooldown.

overview: Strengths: A nimble assassin and sniper who can operate effectively in both close and long-distance combat. Black Widow can switch between her melee and ranged modes freely. Her ranged primary fire, Red Room Rifle, is a single shot that deals high damage. * Her secondary fire activates Straight Shooter, which enters a first-person zoomed view to land more precise shots, which are not affected by damage distance falloff. ** If the player is currently in her melee mode, she will switch automatically to the rifle when activating the secondary fire. Her melee primary fire, Widow's Bite Baton, has her dual-wielding batons to quickly strike at enemies in close range. Edge Dancer is a low-range kick that knocks enemies back in the air; the player can press it again upon a successful hit to grapple towards the same or different target for a follow-up kick. * This is Black Widow's main move to fend off backline divers such as Black Panther, Spider-Man, and Iron Fist. * It can also be used to push enemies off of ledges. Fleet Foot grants her increased movement speed and access to a higher jump, which aids her in repositioning on the battlefield and to also outrun opponents. Her Ultimate, Electro-Plasma Explosion, deals high damage in a large radius, leaves enemies vulnerable for a short duration, and also leaves pools of red plasma on the ground that slows enemies, reducing their movement speed by 55%; this is one the highest slowing percentages in the game. * This synergizes with her primary fire, allowing Black Widow to pin down and more easily eliminate high-priority opponents such as Strategists. Weaknesses: Despite having access to melee options, Black Widow lacks the survivability to stay in the frontline; her melee options must be used sparingly to avoid run-ins with backline divers as much as possible. Has no defensive means to gain bonus health or self-healing. Switching between her weapons can feel a bit counter-intuitive at first to deal with immediate threats; the brief moment when entering into Straight Shooter must be accounted for. Edge Dancer has a very low range and a rather long cooldown. Enemies with high mobility tools can counter the slowing effect of her Ultimate.

paragraphs: Easy Damage/Kill Close-Range Combo with Red Room Rifle: Hip Fire Red Room Rifle, Cancel into Edge Dancer and Hookshot Kick Follow-up Attack, and Hip Fire again while they're stunned. Reload time of Red Room Rifle is accounted for after Hookshot Kick Followup due to the stun. ADS/Straight Shooter can also be used, but is not recommended due to lack of damage fall off as combo does not exceed the 20 meter range for falloff damage to take place. Calculated damage is 310 for the full combo.

overview: Strengths: A versatile Duelist that primarily focuses on attacking at close range and has extra abilities to cover distance from medium range. He can quickly change up his attack style to deal damage and apply debuffs. Has higher max health compared to other Duelists. Blade is able to switch between two primary weapons: * Ancestral Sword is a standard melee weapon with a fast attack speed. * Hunter's Shotgun shoots a projectile that splits into shrapnel after having travelled 8 meters. His secondary fire, Scarlet Shroud, allows Blade to drain resource to block with his sword, granting himself 80% damage reduction from the front. * Blade is also immune to crowd control during the first 0.4 seconds of the block. * For every 200 damage that is blocked, he regains 1 charge for Daywalker Dash. Daywalker Dash causes Blade to surge forward and apply a debuff to the first enemy he collides with. * If the sword is equipped, Blade will perform a cleaving strike that applies stacks of healing reduction each hit. If Bloodline Awakening is active, each hit also grants an attack speed stack. * If the gun is equipped, Blade will shoot a follow-up shot that inflicts slow. Bloodline Awakening allows Blade to toggle an awakened state where he boosts movement speed, gains lifesteal, suffers from healing reduction, and is forced to use his sword. While in this state, he can build up a meter that increases his attack speed with every slash he lands (up to 10 stacks). * Excess healing is converted into bonus health. * While above 5 stacks, Blade will perform a whirlwind slash at the end of every combo. Landing every hit of it will also grant 1 additional stack. * Blade can see low-health enemies through whiles while in this state. His Ultimate, Thousand-Fold Slash, charges up a forward dash that deals damage on impact and then a barrage of laido strikes within the attack range. Enemies hit are inflicted with 40% healing reduction for 4 seconds. * The longer Blade charges up the attack, the longer the dash distance becomes. * Blade also restores health from damage dealt even if Bloodline Awakening is not active. When paired with Moon Knight, Blade gains access to Eclipse Edge. When activated, Blade dashes forward and enables a 3-second combo window to unleash a Moonblade Combo. * Moonblade Combo quickly launches four projectiles that bounce between nearby enemies for damage. Weaknesses: Using the sword without Bloodline Awakening being active proves heavily ineffective. Hunter's Shotgun is a lot more inconsistent in terms of damage compared to other pellet-based weapons. Daywalker Dash's secondary attack can be blocked by barriers/shields. During Bloodline Awakening, the lifesteal is affected by his healing reduction. * Additionally, the whirlwind slash at the end of an attack combo does not grant a stack for each hit.

strategyGuide.sourceTitle: Blade strategy on Marvel Rivals Wiki
strategyGuide.summary: Blade plays as a pressure dealer who stabilizes teammates through burst pressure.
paragraphs: Blade is a deadly brawler whose power steadily increases the longer he fights. While his strikes start off weak, he can quickly become one of the most dangerous up-close fighters in the game the longer he remains left to his own devices. Even outside of that, he can still provide decent ranged damage as well as team utility with a slow and a unique anti-heal property.

overview: Strengths: A unique Vanguard that has two base forms: Bruce Banner and Hero Hulk. * As Bruce Banner, he is very frail and has a limited moveset, but has access to ranged attacks and a knockback ability. However, his Ultimate allows him to transform into the Hulk, his main combat form, with an extremely low energy cost, the lowest of any Ultimate in the game. * As Hulk he serves as a bulky frontliner with high mobility and specialized in melee pressure. If Hulk's health is depleted he reverts back to Bruce Banner, effectively giving him two health bars. Bruce Banner's primary fire is Gamma Ray Gun, which has a high rate-of-fire but deals low damage. However, each successful hit adds Gamma Energy, necessary to transform back into Hero Hulk; headshots grant even more energy. Bruce Banner's Gamma Grenade knocks back enemies, but most importantly creates a spell field on the ground that grants Gamma Energy. * The grenade also deals knockback to Bruce himself, which with a well-timed jump, can be utilized to escape from enemies or to reach high ground (this can be useful for getting out of spawn slightly faster). Bruce Banner always starts matches with his Ultimate, Puny Banner, available, which allows him to transform into the Hulk. During his transformation he is completely immune to all damage and crowd control effects. The Hulk's primary fire is Heavy Blow, quick swings with a decent range and arc to dish out consistent damage. Despite being more suited for melee, the Hulk has access to two projectiles: Gamma Burst, which Hulk performs his signature thunderclap which launches a single shockwave projectile that can pierce enemies in a line; and Radioactive Lockdown, a blast of gamma energy that deals minimal damage but stuns enemies for a few seconds. * Radioactive Lockdown can prevent enemies from capturing objectives as the gauge will not fill if they are stunned. Incredible Leap allows the Hulk to jump over larger distances, the height increasing the longer the move is charged. Additionally, if Hulk jumps directly on a wall, he clings onto it. Lastly, if the leap hits a flying enemy, they will be knocked down and dragged with him. Indestructible Guard projects an energy shield around the Hulk and all nearby allies, preventing and absorbing all damage taken, converting it into Gamma Energy to fuel the Hulk's Ultimate. If the Hulk is damaged while the shield is up, it converts 100% of the damage into Gamma Energy and 10% of the damage from his allies. The Hulk's Ultimate, Hulk Smash, temporarily transforms the Hulk into Monster Hulk, which restores and nearly doubles his max health and enhances his abilities: * Heavy Blow has more range and deals more damage at the expense of being slightly slower. * Gamma Burst's projectile becomes slower, but deals almost double damage and has a much lower cooldown (1 second down from 8 seconds). * Radioactive Lockdown's projectile is faster and has a much lower cooldown (5 seconds down from 15 seconds). * Monster Hulk also instantly gains a new Ultimate, World Breaker, wherein he grabs an enemy and delivers a series of devastating slam attacks for massive damage. During this ability, he gains a 30% damage reduction. Weaknesses: Bruce Banner is incredibly frail, having the lowest max health in the game, on top of having no mobility tools other than with Gamma Grenade, and a very limited moveset. Despite having projectiles, the Hulk is susceptible to enemies with long-distance attacks that can out-zone him. The Hulk often has to rely on grouping up with allies to charge his Ultimate more efficiently, due to Indestructible Guard's mechanics. As Monster Hulk, he loses access to Indestructible Guard. The Hulk's large frame and average movement speed makes him an easy target, and even more so in his Monster Hulk form. The Hulk is vulnerable to crowd control while Indestructible Guard is on cooldown.

paragraphs: Hulk is a high-risk, high-reward tank who can quickly shift between harassing the enemy's backline and protecting his own on a whim. His high mobility allows him to quickly engage and disengage the enemy, giving him free reign to take or abandon almost any fight with enough foresight.

overview: Strengths: A sturdy and mobile diver that can storm the battlefield, specialized in hit & run tactics and capable of shielding himself to protect his allies. His primary fire, Sentinel Strike, which can launch his shield every two punches landed. * This allows Captain America to keep enemies under constant pressure. His secondary fire, Living Legend, allows Captain America to block oncoming strikes and projectiles while moving at a slightly slower speed. * Projectiles can be deflected at 50% damage, including Ultimates, such as Iron Man's Invincible Pulse Cannon. * The shield is able to deflect the enemy team's Jeff the Land Shark's Healing Bubbles if they're shot directly at Captain America, which allows them to be picked by him and his allies instead. * The shield can completely block certain area-of-effect Ultimates, such as Namor's Horn of Proteus, if the player aims the shield directly down. Vibranium Energy Saw allows him to manually throw his shield, which can ricochet at enemies close to each other, capable of disrupting abilities if timed appropriately. * In addition to Sentinel Strike's shield throw, this allows Captain America to effectively harass the enemy backline. Leading Dash grants him 33% increased movement speed and access to a higher jump called Fearless Leap. * When jumping with Leading Dash active, Captain gains access to Super-Soldier Slam, a diving attack that knocks enemies up. Liberty Rush is a dashing charge with his shield that deals damage and also allows him to quickly reposition in the battlefield. His Ultimate, Freedom Charge, grants bonus health and an additional 30% movement speed to Captain America and all nearby allies, making him very resilient and hard to take down. * Using Living Legend or Leading Dash while this ability is active can knock enemies upwards. Weaknesses: Living Legend has a 1 second cooldown after the player pulls the shield down, which can interrupt the flow of his combat a bit. Struggles to block large barrages of ranged damage due to the smaller size of his shield compared to the likes of Doctor Strange's Shield of the Seraphim and Magneto's Metallic Curtain. His shield cannot block splash damage, such as Iron Man's Repulsor Blast and Jeff the Land Shark's Aqua Burst. He can, however, deflect those moves if they're aimed directly at him. The player has no control of where deflected projectiles will ricochet to.

paragraphs: Captain America is a hyper-aggressive dive tank who excels at diving in and out of combat and being a constant nuisance for the enemy backline. While his damage output may seem low on paper, his incredible mobility allows him to easily weave in and out of danger while still getting hits in, allowing his damage to add up significantly over time as he looks for an opportune moment to strike.

buildProfileRationale.reason: Cloak & Dagger was scored from Fandom overview text, listed strengths/weaknesses, ability descriptions, and 0 technical detail entries. Strategist role weighting sets the baseline, then descriptions adjust Utility, Damage, Sustain, Mobility, and Ultimate independently. 0 ultimate-labeled ability entryies and 0 passive entryies influenced the final profile.

overview: Dagger's Strengths: Her primary fire, Lightforce Dagger, is a quick projectile that bounces on terrain. * This ability can both heal allies or damage enemies, and has a semi-tracking feature which will cause the projectile to hit the ally or enemy closest to the aiming reticle. ** Four small white ticks appear around her allies or enemies to visually show where the projectiles will home in. ** There is a circle that appears when she uses it, healing the person she was aiming for, nearby allies and herself. Her secondary fire, Dagger Storm, launches a projectile that creates a large bubble of light on impact, providing healing-over-time. * This is Dagger's primary tool of self-sustain. * This ability can be attached to any surface, including walls and ceilings. * The healing bubble persists even if the player switches to Cloak. Light Explosion creates a bright white, rectangular spell field that advances in a straight line along the casting path, healing all allies caught by it. * This ability also provides a 15% healing bonus from all sources for 4 seconds when hitting allies. Shadow's Embrace allows Dagger to switch to Cloak. * Doing this automatically reloads Lightforce Dagger's ammo when swapping back to Dagger. Their Ultimate, Eternal Bond, has both Cloak & Dagger perform four rapid spinning dashes along the ground, leaving a persistent energy carpet that simultaneously heals allies and damages enemies that step over it. * Players can change the direction of this ability between the dash pauses. * Each individual energy carpet lasts for 5 seconds. Dagger's Weaknesses: Susceptible to dives as she lacks any form of mobility outside of her Ultimate. Lightforce Dagger does very little damage; she relies on Cloak to ward off aggressive enemies. Veil of Lightforce does not pass through walls or other terrain. Dagger Storm is one of the only abilities that can heal her, as if she is getting dived and no one is near, she cannot use Lightforce Daggers healing circle, and she is very vulnerable if she gets pushed or pulled out. She can still be damaged and taken down during Eternal Bond, especially by high burst damage. * In addition, on certain maps with high terrain inclinations, Cloak & Dagger may get stuck during the dashes. Cloak's Strengths: His primary fire, Darkforce Cloak, is a channeled energy beam that deals high consecutive damage. * This ability has infinite ammo and the same auto-aim effect as Dagger's; Cloak will automatically attack the nearest enemy within its radius. His secondary fire, Dark Teleportation, creates a large energy field around Cloak that temporarily phases him and all nearby allies, making them invisible to enemies and immune to all damage and crowd control effects. * Cloak and his allies also gain movement speed while within the energy field. * Cloak gains a limited form of free-flight during this ability. * The invulnerability granted by this ability can even avoid Ultimates, such as Iron Man's Invincible Pulse Cannon and Jeff the Land Shark's It's Jeff! Terror Cape, which visually appears similar to Dagger's Veil of Lightforce, creates a dark purple, rectangular spell field that advances in a straight line along the casting path, obstructing enemy vision. * This ability also inflicts enemies with the Vulnerability debuff, which amplifies all damage taken by 20% for 3 seconds. Light's Embrace allows Cloak to switch to Dagger. His Ultimate is the same as when controlling Dagger, with the same attributes. Cloak's Weaknesses: Darkforce Cloak has limited range, requiring Cloak to get into mid-close range. This, combined with his low max health, can make it risky to engage directly against enemies without the rest of the team. Cannot heal his team. He must switch back to Dagger to provide defensive support. Very vulnerable if Dark Teleportation is on cooldown. * In addition, this ability's effects only last for 2 seconds. Terror Cape's blinding effect only lasts for 1.5 seconds.

buildProfileRationale.reason: Daredevil was scored from Fandom overview text, listed strengths/weaknesses, ability descriptions, and 0 technical detail entries. Duelist role weighting sets the baseline, then descriptions adjust Utility, Damage, Sustain, Mobility, and Ultimate independently. 0 ultimate-labeled ability entryies and 0 passive entryies influenced the final profile.

overview: Strengths: A combo-orientated Duelist with great potential for mobility and excellent means of both defense and offense. He can cycle between his abilities to maximize his damage output. Can scale walls and cling to them. Blind beyond a certain distance, making enemies within that range invisible to the player. His passive, Radar Sense, detects nearby enemies and allies through walls. Daredevil uses Fury as a resource, holding up to three charges that are gained through abilities. His primary fire, Justice Jab, is a basic melee strike that grants 0.5 Fury per hit. * After using Devil's Latch or Infernal Fury, he enables a 5s window of time to use Righteous Cross as his next attack - where he lunges forward for one heavy strike and gains 0.5 Fury on hit. His secondary fire, Objection, has Daredevil enter a defensive stance for up to 1.5s where he deflects attacks and projectiles from the front. * He also gains 2 Fury after the ability ends if he deflected at least one attack. Devil's Latch is a grapple ability that grants 1 Fury upon completion and enables Righteous Cross. * If the grapple hits an enemy, the target will be reeled in; which is great for closing distance and preventing opponents from escaping. Infernal Fury consumes 2 Fury to unleash either Devil's Chain or Devil's Throw, both of which enable Righteous Cross afterward. * Devil's Chain is a close-range strike that grants 25 bonus health on use and additional bonus health based on damage dealt. If an enemy is hit within the strikespot at the end of the attack range, they take nearly double the damage, granting you more bonus health. * Devil's Throw hurls the batons to bounce between walls and nearby enemies, slowing down targets upon hit. Daredevil also gains 50 bonus health when this attack is used. * Devil's Chain is great for sustain during one-on-one fights, while Devil's Throw is better for 'accidental picks' and damage spread. Sonic Pursuit marks a target and grants Daredevil damage reduction and a movement speed boost (which decays the farther he is from the marked opponent). * Daredevil can also dash to marked enemies, blinding them and granting himself 2 Fury upon completion. * If an opponent is KO'd within a few seconds of being marked, the dash cooldown will also be reset. This is how you can bounce between opponents quickly and still have the resources to play aggressive. His Ultimate, Let The Devil Out, creates a visible dome around the player that ramps up a damage over time and blindness effect for enemies within line of sight. While active, you continously regain Fury as well. Weaknesses: Since the brawn of his kit ties into Infernal Fury, Daredevil becomes extremely vulnerable when he has drained all his Fury and cooldowns. Devil's Latch and Sonic Pursuit can be disrupted by earthbound before either ability is completed, causing you not to gain Fury afterward. Let The Devil Out is rather situational since it reveals your location while active, making you a high-priority target if your own team is not able to assist you. Inputs can feel rather sticky because of his aggressive playstyle.

playstyles.title: Rank One Flank Tank Pool
playstyles.summary: Play Tank Pool as a chaos-control flanker: stage from off-angles, pressure squishies and isolated backline targets, then escape with upgraded movement before the enemy can collapse on you.
playstyles.whenToUse: Use this style when the map gives flank routes, backline access, or isolated targets. It is strongest when your team can hold the front while you force attention from behind or the side.

coreIdeas.label: Play like a disruptive off-tank, not a main wall
coreIdeas.description: The reviewed rank-one Tank Pool rarely frontlines. He repeatedly stages behind the enemy team, forces supports and DPS to look away from the main fight, then leaves before the punish arrives.

coreIdeas.label: Upgrade movement early when flanking
coreIdeas.description: The double-dash upgrade is a first or early priority because this style needs two exits. The extra dash lets Deadpool take deeper backline angles without instantly exploding.

coreIdeas.label: Use guns as the default pressure tool
coreIdeas.description: Most neutral pressure comes from Dual Desert Eagles. The review notes that the player uses guns far more often than swords, especially for poking, finishing backline targets, and helping against threats other tanks cannot easily reach.

coreIdeas.label: Swap to swords for close rooms and survival reactions
coreIdeas.description: Swords are mainly used when targets are close enough that aiming becomes awkward, when the sword ultimate is the better finisher, or when a dodge/survival reaction is needed, such as hearing Jeff ultimate.

coreIdeas.label: Use Deadpool In Your Area before backline commits
coreIdeas.description: Pop E before jumping the backline for the attack-speed window and surprise burst, then use the zone to split supports from each other or isolate a target from healing.

coreIdeas.label: Choose kills over photos when the fight is already won
coreIdeas.description: Going back for the photo is not always correct. If the enemy has backed far enough away or your team can finish the fight, keep pressure and secure the win condition before resetting for style points.

coreIdeas.label: B-hop and small movement tech matter
coreIdeas.description: The review calls out frequent bunny hopping and dash timing as important Deadpool habits. Dash before certain ult sequences when possible because ult refreshes cooldowns, giving more chances to complete movement hits.

upgradeNotes: Common flank order: double dash early, then Dual Desert Eagles, then Deadpool In Your Area.
The order changes by map and enemy threats. Into Punisher or long-range pressure, the reviewed player sometimes upgrades gun ultimate earlier.
Bubble/shield is used more defensively before it is upgraded; aggressive early bubbles were called punishable.
ultimateNotes: Gun ultimate is preferred often when a specific ranged target must be checked, such as Punisher or Phoenix.
Sword ultimate is strong in close fights, chase windows, and multi-target cleanup, but the better ultimate choice changes by situation rather than one ultimate being always correct.
Angela can be a valuable Ban Hammer target when she is low or shifting because she can still die during the shift interaction.

playstyles.title: Jackpot Combo DPS Pool
playstyles.summary: Play DPS Pool as a high-tempo combo diver: farm upgrades quickly, pressure in gun form, then swap into katana form for dash resets, bunny-hop chains, and Jackpot-driven ultimate extensions.
playstyles.whenToUse: Use this style when you can survive aggressive mechanics-heavy engages and your team can support a Duelist who spends fights bouncing between forms. It shines when you can repeatedly build ult, force chaos, and convert one target before resetting.

coreIdeas.label: Farm the resource loop before hard committing
coreIdeas.description: DPS Pool gets scary when upgrades and ultimate come online. Use early pressure, safe pokes, teammate/enemy bounce opportunities, and photos after kills to accelerate the upgrade bar instead of diving with no plan.

coreIdeas.label: Open with gun pressure, then swap to katana for the commit
coreIdeas.description: A key sequence is throwing the gun-form right click, swapping to katana, then letting the projectile continue its value while you chase with sword pressure. This keeps damage active during the form swap instead of wasting the setup.

coreIdeas.label: Katana form is about right-click resets and bounce discipline
coreIdeas.description: Once you commit, the core pattern becomes right click, space, left click, and repeat. Landing the dash/reset matters more than mashing randomly, because missed resets turn DPS Pool into an easy feed.

coreIdeas.label: Jackpot is the real win condition in ultimate
coreIdeas.description: During the DPS ultimate, keep bouncing and landing hits to reach the five-count Jackpot. Jackpot adds the big fight swing: more duration, more healing, more speed, and the ability to keep the pressure going long enough to turn the point.

coreIdeas.label: Use photos as resource conversion, not only style
coreIdeas.description: Photos can give a large upgrade payout after kills. Take them when the fight state allows it, but do not throw away tempo if you still need to chase, touch, or finish the fight.

coreIdeas.label: The style is powerful but very easy to feed on
coreIdeas.description: This version of Deadpool asks for excellent tracking, target awareness, and movement control while the screen is messy. If you enter without cooldowns, miss the bounce chain, or chase past support range, you can disappear instantly.

coreIdeas.label: Tracking during chaos separates the ceiling from the average
coreIdeas.description: The strongest DPS Pool examples keep aim stable while bouncing, dashing, swapping forms, and chasing airborne targets. Mechanical control is the reason the style can look simple while actually being extremely difficult.

upgradeNotes: Common early priorities are the dash/right-click tools and gun pressure tools because they start the resource and chase loop.
Watch for the visual upgrade indicators and gold weapon states so you know which form has been powered up.
The best upgrade order changes by round and objective state, but DPS Pool should prioritize the pieces that let him farm ultimate, chase safely, and keep resets active.
ultimateNotes: DPS ultimate is strongest when you can farm Jackpot instead of using it as a panic button.
In sword ultimate, keep landing bounce/right-click hits to extend the window and sustain through pressure.
A good DPS Pool ultimate can flip a point by itself if Jackpot is reached and the enemy cannot break the reset chain.

overview: Strengths: A versatile hybrid ranged and melee combatant who can upgrade his abilities mid-match, and can be played as a Vanguard, Duelist or Strategist, allowing him to fit into any team-composition. * Each one of Deadpool's roles technically count as a separate character. ** Deadpool has different stats per role. ** In the Competitive Mode, each Deadpool role must be banned separately. Deadpool has two passives: * Healing Factor provides Deadpool with a healing-over-time effect of 25 per second if he has been out of combat for 5 seconds. ** This passive will automatically activate if he takes more than 200 damage within a 2-second window; during this, the effect is 50 per second instead. He will also become immune to damage for a brief duration, making him practically immune to one-shot abilities. This has a 45 second cooldown. * Maximum Flair is tied to Deadpool's Ultimate activation; unlike other characters, he doesn't generate Ultimate charge over-time, instead gainining "Style Points" depending on his actions. Whenever he hits an enemy or heals an ally, he gains Style Points, starting on on rank E, and once it reaches rank S, he can activate his Ultimate. ** He gains the most amount of Style Points when he lands his abilities in quick succession. Actions taken in combat grant Deadpool experience points, shown as a red gauge underneath his health; once this gauge is filled, Deadpool can upgrade one of his abilities, empowering them. * Although ability upgrades are unique to each role, Deadpool's experience gauge is shared across all three variants. This means that if a player swaps roles mid-match, the specific ability upgrades will reset, but all earned experience is retained, allowing the player to immediately re-allocate their upgrade points to the new role's skills. Deadpool has two primary weapons in all three of his roles: * Dual Desert Eagles fire hitscan bullets with moderate damage and fast attack speed. ** When upgraded, every shot charges up an icon that once full, will launch an explosive projectile with a slow projectile speed. Deadpool also gains increased max ammo. * Kick@$$ Katanas is a melee weapon with a very fast attack speed. The second strike is a fast jab attack that can easily prove to be the brute of his damage. ** When upgraded, the jab strike can deal critical damage. Bouncing off an enemy with the double jump will guarentee a jab strike as well, enhancing his offensive capabilities. Deadpool In Your Area is an ability for all roles: Deadpool creates a zone around himself for 5 seconds that clutters the screen of nearby enemies and deals continous damage. The taunt effect is removed once Deadpool suffers enough damage. * When upgraded, for all roles, enemies have their health bar taped over if they stay in the area for too long. Deadpool can choose to take a selfie with enemies he lands a final blow on before they respawn, granting additional XP. While doing so, Deadpool cannot be damaged and is immune to crowd control. Vanguard: = As a Vanguard, Deadpool trades in high damage/healing output for greater survivability and team utility, gaining double the max health. His secondary fire with the desert eagles, Magical Unicorn Shield, lobs a plushie barrier at the target location that can be used to block enemy fire. * Similarly to other shield-type abilities, Magical Unicorn Shield can block enemy line-of-sight, as well sustain damage from any ability in the game, including Ultimate abilities such as Iron Man's Invincible Iron Cannon and Scarlet Witch's Reality Erasure. * When upgraded, the plushie becomes golden; gaining increased health and size. His secondary fire with the katanas, Hazardous Hijinx, has Deadpool dash forward and slash enemies upon collision. On hit, a 5-second window is enabled where this ability can be chained up to two more times before going on cooldown. * When upgraded, this ability gains an additional charge and Deadpool will bounce in the air after landing a hit. Deadpool In Your Area grants increased attack speed to Deadpool and nearby allies. * When upgraded, the ability also provides damage reduction (40% to Deadpool, and 20% to affected allies). Bunny Bounce is a basic double jump with a 6 second cooldown. * The cooldown is instantly refreshes when the player jumps off a wall or enemy while The Big Test is active. His Ultimate with the desert eagles, The Ban Hammer, taunts a targeted enemy with an icon that will damage them every time they miss an attack; while Deadpool gains bonus health and healing over time. The player even gains additional bonus health when the target misses. * When upgraded, the enemy will also take additional damage every second. His Ultimate with the katanas, The Big Test, grants speed, healing over time, bonus health for allies, and intiating a challenge for Deadpool to land Bunny Bounce or Hazardous Hijinx five times within 12s. Once he does, he lands the Jackpot; doubling speed, healing, and refreshing the cooldown of Healing Factor. * Hazardous Hijinx has all charges refreshed once the challenge is initiated. * When upgraded, Deadpool grants bonus health to himself and nearby allies by dealing damage with Jackpot active. Duelist: = As a Duelist, Deadpool loses survivability and utility for raw damage output. His secondary fire with the desert eagles, Headshot!, throws out his avatar icon out to spin in place for continous damage, then retract back to him. * When upgraded, Deadpool will throw his avatar three times total; causing it to explode on the last throw. * Upgrading this ability early-on is a great way to XP as quick as possible. His secondary fire with the katanas is Hazardous Hijinx, which has two charges even without upgrades. * When upgraded, the ability can be chained an unlimited amount of times; bouncing after each hit. Deadpool In Your Area grants 20% damage reduction to himself. Bunny Hop can be reset by bouncing off a wall or enemy. Deadpool also gains bonus health when doing so. His Ultimate with the desert eagles, Skill Issue, taunts an enemy to suffer damage for every attack they miss. * When upgraded, the enemy will take additional damage every second and be inflicted with a stack of Vulnerability on each missed attack (10% per stack, up to 4 stacks). His Ultimate with the katanas, Pop Quiz!, grants speed, healing over time, and initiates a challenge for Deadpool to land Bunny Hop or Hazardous Hijinx seven times within 12s. Once he does, he receives double the speed and healing from landing the Jackpot. * When upgraded, Deadpool also gains a 15% damage boost that doubles when he completes the challenge. * Landing the Jackpot will also reset all cooldowns except for his Healing Factor. Strategist: = As a Strategist, Deadpool exchanges offensive capabilities for healing and team support. Dual Desert Eagles can heal allies, while Kick@$$ Katanas heals nearby allies after every hit. * When upgraded, the explosive projectile from his desert eagles also heals allies caught in the blast. His secondary fire with the desert eagles, Bouncing Bobblehead, is the same as Headshot except the avatar icon also heals allies - both whilst spinning and on the explosion. * Upgrading this ability early-on is a great way to XP as quick as possible. His secondary fire with the katanas, Healing Hijinx, is a short dash attack that heals nearby allies on impact. * When upgraded, Deadpool can chain the dash up to three total times. He also bounces after each hit. Deadpool In Your Area heals those nearby while active. * When upgraded, the healing/damage is increased while allies gain a damage boost. Healing Hop restores health for allies you bounce off of, resetting the cooldown. His Ultimate with the desert eagles, Pwnage Pound, is the same taunt that grants healing over time on activation and additional healing for each attack missed. * When upgraded, the target suffers extra damage every second. His Ultimate with the katanas, Final Exam, applies healing over time and initiates a challenge for Deadpool to land Healing Hop, Healing Hijinx, or Bouncing Bobblehead eight times within 12s. Once he does, he lands the Jackpot and earns greater healing. * When upgraded, landing the Jackpot grants bonus health and refreshes the Healing Factor cooldown. Weaknesses: If Deadpool is defeated, his style gauge is reset to rank E, even if he reached rank S and charged his Ultimate. This means that he's the only character in the game that can lose his Ultimate once charged. While Deadpool charges his Ultimate faster than other heroes, it does have a cooldown of 60 seconds after each use. This makes his Ultimates favor frequency over effectiveness. As a Vanguard, Deadpool struggles to gain XP the most between his two other roles. As a Duelist, his utility can often feel outclassed by the Vanguard role. * Upgraded Hazardous Hijinx will also not begin to recharge until Deadpool misses a dash. As a Strategist, he heavily relies upon his team to support him while his Ultimate is active to make large plays.

overview: Strengths: An extremely bulky, self-sustaining Vanguard who uses powerful melee attacks to inflict damage over time and generate bonus health by doing so. Devil Dinosaur has two passives: * Prehistoric Plating reduces damage taken on a critical hit by 50%, makes you immune to displacement effects, and reduces the Ultimate charge that enemies/allies gain by damaging/healing you by 35%. * Apex Advantage converts 150% of the damage you deal to bleeding enemies into bonus health. His primary fire, Primal Bite, performs a slow but high-damage melee strike that inflicts bleeding on hit. * Bleeding effects last for 4s and cause targets to take 1.5% of their max health as damage every 0.5s. His secondary fire, Impact Beam, drains a resource meter to fire a damaging beam of energy. It inflicts bleeding and slow to enemies it hits, and knocks down airborne targets. Savage Predation has Devil leap into the air, dealing damage and inflicting bleeding upon landing. * If Devil collides with an already-bleeding enemy during the leap, the ability ends and he clamps down on them; allowing Devil to briefly carry the target in his mouth before throwing them forward. Buddy Barrier summons a spherical barrier around yourself that has 450 HP and lasts for up to 6s. Enemies caught within the inside of the barrier are also slowed. Frenzied Feast has Devil perform a flurry of bites, the fifth being a heavier bite that lunges you forward. All damage dealt is converted into bonus health. * Devil also deals additional percentage damage to bleeding targets. This allows him to rack up an extremely high amount of bonus health when attacking high-health heroes such as other Vanguards. His Ultimate, Devil-Beast Rampage, empowers Devil for 10s; and while active, Devil greatly boosts his movement speed and unleashes shockwaves that inflict bleeding and knock back opponents. Savage Predation and Frenzied Feast have reduced cooldowns while active. * Upon activation, Devil gains a large sum of bonus health and emits a large sonic wave projectile that slows enemies upon hit. When paired with The Punisher, Frank can mount Devil to ride on top of him, forcing him to use only Adjudication/Deliverance and Scourge Grenade; but gaining damage reduction. * Devil absorbs a portion of the damage and healing that Frank receives while active. Weaknesses: Due to its large size, Devil Dinosaur may have movement problems in specific areas, with some maps even having sections where it is impossible for him to pass. Devil's head hitbox is quite wide, so despite his passive, heroes who benefit from landing critical hits like Mantis or Phoenix are strong adversaries against him. Can prove powerful in one-versus-one scenarios but is extremely weak against larger crowds of enemies.

name: Doctor Strange
summary: As the Sorcerer Supreme, Doctor Stephen Strange gracefully wields ancient spells to turn the tide of even the most impossible battle. However, magic always comes at a cost, and each use of his arcane abilities gradually awakens the darkness within him.
playstyle: Use Doctor Strange as a Vanguard around cover, cooldown timing, and team follow-up.

buildProfileRationale.reason: Doctor Strange was scored from Fandom overview text, listed strengths/weaknesses, ability descriptions, and 0 technical detail entries. Vanguard role weighting sets the baseline, then descriptions adjust Utility, Damage, Sustain, Mobility, and Ultimate independently. 0 ultimate-labeled ability entryies and 0 passive entryies influenced the final profile.

overview: Strengths: A versatile mixed attacker with a flexible kit fit to adapt to different situations, being able to both serve as a stalwart protector as well as a powerful disruptor. He shines in defensive objectives. His primary fire, Daggers of Denak, are relatively slow-moving, shotgun-esque projectiles. There is a small delay in-between shots. * An advanced technique to frame-cancel the endlag is to weave the primary fire with his melee attack. This is especially useful during close combat skirmishes to increase Doctor Strange's burst damage significantly. ** This weaving technique also allows Doctor Strange to gather more Dark Magic stacks faster. His secondary fire, Shield of the Seraphim, projects a large radial shield in front of him that absorbs all incoming projectiles while moving at a slower speed. * This ability can even block certain Ultimates, such as the blasts shot by Hela's Goddess of Death. * Doctor Strange can use this ability while flying with his Cloak of Levitation. * If timed correctly, the player can use the ability to frame-cancel Doctor Strange's primary reload animation, reducing its duration by roughly one-third. Maelstrom of Madness is an ability that interacts directly with his passive, Price of Magic. Each time Doctor Strange deals damage with his primary fire or melee attack, he gains stacks of Dark Magic, up to 100. If he reaches the maximum amount of Dark Magic and does not release it after 3 seconds, he is afflicted by the Anti-Heal curse, which makes him unable to be healed for a few seconds. The stacks of Dark Magic that Doctor Strange possesses visually appear as a glowing purple symbol on his cape that becomes more pronounced as more stacks are accumulated. * Dark Magic decays after a few seconds if the player doesn't deal damage. Decay is stopped immediately if further damage is done (excluding decay from max percentage.) * Doctor Strange gains 3.5 Dark Magic if he hits enemies with Daggers of Denak, and 10 Dark Magic with his melee attack. * The player can release the Dark Magic by using Maelstrom of Madness, which deals more damage the more Dark Magic he currently has (1.2 damage per stack). Pentagram of Farallah allows Doctor Strange to open two portals, one from his current location and another to anywhere on the map. Alternatively, the first portal's position can be repositioned as well, allowing both portals to be placed wherever. Both allies and enemies can use the portals, which last for 20 seconds. All projectiles will travel through the portal, including Ultimates. * If Doctor Strange channels the portal spell while in mid-air, he will remain floating indefinitely until he places the other portal. His Ultimate, Eye of Agamotto, is a powerful crowd control tool. Doctor Strange releases a blast in a large radius around him that temporarily stuns all enemies within range for 3 seconds, projecting their souls outside of their bodies. Players can deal damage to these souls which will reflect the damage into their physical bodies. Weaknesses: Limited mobility due to the long cooldown of Cloak of Levitation. Vulnerable if his Shield of the Seraphim is destroyed. Shield of the Seraphim cannot block melee attacks. When Dark Magic begins to gradually decrease from its maximum 100 amount, it cannot be regained until it is released by Maelstrom of Madness or upon reaching zero stacks. Pentagram of Farallah has a very long cooldown, which will trigger if the player cancels the portal or if he is hit at all while he is casting. * In addition, projectiles can only travel through the portals once per cast. Maelstrom of Madness relies on the player doing constant damage to build-up Dark Magic, and being within melee range to use it, which Doctor Strange is not a specialist at.

strategyGuide.sourceTitle: Doctor Strange strategy on Marvel Rivals Wiki
strategyGuide.summary: Doctor Strange plays as a space-maker. Key tools: Cloak of Levitation, Maelstrom of Madness.
paragraphs: Before using Doctor Strange's ultimate ability, players can charge their Maelstrom of Madness with Dark Magic, then use Cloak of Levitation to fly above the enemy team. Afterwards, players can activate their ultimate ability while canceling their flight to use Doctor Strange's ultimate abilities range to its fullest. After the ultimate ability animation has ended, players can use their charged up Dark Magic to deal heavy damage on their stunned foes.

buildProfileRationale.reason: Elsa Bloodstone was scored from Fandom overview text, listed strengths/weaknesses, ability descriptions, and 0 technical detail entries. Duelist role weighting sets the baseline, then descriptions adjust Utility, Damage, Sustain, Mobility, and Ultimate independently. 0 ultimate-labeled ability entryies and 0 passive entryies influenced the final profile.

overview: Strengths: All-rounder combatant that slowly ramps up her survivability and damage potential over time. Many of her abilities have a short cooldown and/or can be used to make for quick eliminations on vulnerable opponents. Her passive, Inherited Instinct, charges Instinct through damage dealt as displayed by a orange bar at the bottom of the player interface. For every level of Instinct she has, the cooldown of Helix Advance is reduced. * Instinct does not decay and is reduced by two levels when Elsa is eliminated. * Elsa gains one level of Instinct when earning a KO and two levels when KO'ing an enemy caught by Smoky Snare. Elsa Bloodstone has two different versions of her primary fire: * Double-Barrel Blaster is her default weapon, which is a shotgun that fires two rounds in quick succession. * Monster-Piercer fires one heavy shotgun blast from her elephant gun. The bullets pierce through enemies and deal percentage damage. Her secondary fire, Living Bullet, shoots a round that splits into seeking shards on impact. Enemies hit by the shards are marked and grant Elsa a 40% movespeed boost for 1s when hit by either of her guns. * Shards deal extra damage to barriers and enemies with bonus health. Helix Advance is a basic dash that grants bonus health. It also enables a 6-second window where Elsa's next primary fire will be Monster-Piercer. * At max Instinct, the cooldown goes as low as 4s, granting you the means of on-demand bonus health and cycling back to your elephant gun for maximum damage output. Ruthless Pursuit performs a dash then launches enemies into the air. Elsa can then reactivate this ability within a 5-second window to fire a follow-up shot. It propels the player back & slows down opponents. * Also grants bonus health upon activation. Both the dash and follow-up shot enable the window for Monster-Piercer. Smoky Snare deploys a small trap that becomes invisible and invincible after 1s of being on the ground. When stepped over, the target is briefly immobilized while Elsa is able to perform a targeted dash. * Elsa also gains two levels of Instinct when eliminating a trapped enemy. * This ability proves effective for locking down flank routes and further hindering enemies caught by a pull. Her Ultimate, Apex Predator, summons the Glartrox to charge forward and drag along enemies its his path then bite down for damage once he reaches the end of its travel distance. * Elsa can reactivate the Ultimate to recall the Glartrox to her position, allowing her to quickly combo it with Relentless Pursuit for devastating damage. With Smoky Snare, Ruthless Pursuit's first dash, and her ultimate Apex Predator, Elsa has a wide variety of crowd control resources that, combined with her great mobility, are very useful for effectively taking down specific enemies. When paired with Deadpool, Elsa's Living Bullet is upgraded into Bloodstone's Boombox Bullets. Marked enemies are slowed, taunted, and suffer from continous damage. Elsa can then recall her rounds to restore missing health based on the damage dealt. Weaknesses: Helix Advance does not provide any vertical mobility. Smoky Snare has a very short deployment range and only immobilizes opponents for 1.2 seconds. * The effect can also be ended early if the hand that appears above a target is killed, since it only has 50 HP. Glartrox can be killed before he performs the bite attack since it only has 400 HP.

paragraphs: Elsa’s primary fire, Double-Barrel Blaster, has a limited magazine of eight rounds fired in two-shot bursts—technically giving you only four shots before needing to reload. Because of this, trigger discipline is paramount; you must make every burst count to maintain pressure during a duel.

name: Emma Frost
summary: For Emma Frost, war is the purest form of art. With her formidable telepathic abilities, she intricately weaves a deadly mental web that ensnares her foes, while her indestructible diamond form lets her lead her teammates fearlessly into the fray. Forever elegant and composed, Emma reigns as the one true queen of the battlefield!

buildProfileRationale.reason: Emma Frost was scored from Fandom overview text, listed strengths/weaknesses, ability descriptions, and 0 technical detail entries. Vanguard role weighting sets the baseline, then descriptions adjust Utility, Damage, Sustain, Mobility, and Ultimate independently. 0 ultimate-labeled ability entryies and 0 passive entryies influenced the final profile.

overview: Strengths: A striking mid-range vanguard with abilities that can give opportunities to deal significant damage as well as being able to transform into a resistant diamond form that offers stronger close range attacks towards enemies. Her primary fire, Telepathic Pulse, is a mid-range beam that deals damage up to 15m. It is a continuous beam of damage that also can deal more damage the more damage dealt with it. * Telepathic Pulse has a meter that rises when dealing damage, the higher the number the higher the damage and when maxxed out to 100 it deals significant damage. * Telepathic Pulse has a max usage of 70 ammo but Emma can use this ability for a significant duration before needing to regenerate this attack. Her secondary fire, Mind's Aegis, creates a diamond shield in front of Emma and the longer it's held down the more distance the shield can be placed at. * Mind's Aegis has 400 health and has infinite duration until destroyed or reclaimed. * Mind's Aegis can be placed at a long distance and thus can be used to defend allies further ahead or be used to block damage coming from a doorway or entrance. * Mind's Aegis shield is stationary once placed and can also be recalled similar to Invisible Woman's Guardian Shield ability. * This shield can block Ultimates such as Iron Man's Invincible Pulse Cannon and Hela's blast shots from Goddess of Death. Psychic Spear is an ability that allows Emma to shoot a psionic projectile that, once hitting an enemy, creates a crystal formation of the enemy's sentience that projects forward towards Emma. The damage dealt to this formation reflects onto the enemy. * When the crystal sentience of the enemy is destroyed before it disappears will deal significant damage to the enemy. * Telepathic Pulse can pierce through the crystal sentience allowing for Emma to deal double damage if the enemy and the crystal sentience are in the same line of fire of her beam. Diamond Form is an ability Emma can use to transform her body into diamond which disables her access to her psychic abilities, however, granting her access to strong counter abilities and passives. Diamond Form lasts for 8 seconds. * In Diamond Form, Emma's primary fire is replaced with Faceted Fury a strong melee attack. * In Diamond Form, Emma can use Carbon Crush allowing her to dash forward a certain distance and when connected to an enemy she will grab them by the throat and slam them down behind her while dealing damage, turning herself around in the process. ** Carbon Crush stuns enemies and can be used to cancel enemy Ultimates during their start up. ** Carbon Crush allows Emma to dash into the sky and can grapple enemies above if they're within range. * In Diamond Form, Emma can use Crystal Kick allowing her to knockback an enemy a certain distance away dealing lots of damage. ** If an enemy is hit with the Crystal Kick into a wall or solid surface, they will receive extra damage hitting the wall. * In Diamond Form, Emma has strong resistance to all forms of damage, and gains Unstoppable; complete immunity from displacements such as stuns, knockbacks, and Jeff the Land Shark's Ultimate It's Jeff!. Her Ultimate, Psionic Seduction, Emma can project a large cone of psionic waves that deal damage overtime. The longer enemies remain within this psionic wave they will be able to be mind controlled, this is indicated by a diamond meter above each enemy's head. * When mind controlled via Psionic Seduction, enemies don't have access to their movement and will instead be lured towards Emma until they die or the ultimate ends. * When mind controlled via Psionic Seduction, enemies within their Ultimate can have their Ultimate cancelled. Weaknesses: Her main attack Telepathic Pulse has a limited range of 15m which requires her to be mid-range to deal damage against enemies. Diamond Form also has no ranged attacks so whenever players utilize this form it is advised to do it when in melee range., unless they're within range of her Carbon Crush ability. Diamond Form's abilities have cooldowns allowing only one or two uses of Carbon Crush and Crystal Kick each within this form. While Diamond Form grants Emma strong resistance, she still takes damage and can be killed within this state. Emma is still susceptible to be killed by Iron Man's Invincible Pulse Cannon. Due to Emma not having any abilities that allow her to move faster outside her Carbon Crush within Diamond Form, she does not have fast mobility and thus can be easily overwhelmed if being chased or surrounded by enemies. Because Mind's Aegis has a shield health of 400, if deployed this shield can be immediately destroyed when facing against significant damage and line of fire from multiple enemies.

overview: Strengths: An agile resource-based support that can match damage output with healing output, utility and debuffs. In turn for long cooldowns, he can support his team by destroying enemy positioning and increase survival through cleansing negative effects. His kit revolves around his Sleight of Hand resource, which can be consumed to grant temporary passive bonuses, as well combined with different card inputs to activate empowerment abilities. * Sleight of Hand has a total of four stacks that regenerates over-time. His primary fire, Kinetic Cards, throws a short spread of three projectiles that explode on contact, damaging enemies while healing allies. His secondary fire, Bayou Bash, creates an burst of energy in front of Gambit that damages enemies and heals allies within the blast radius. * This ability has two charges and can self-heal, making it more effective to repel against flanking enemies. Cajun Charge is a basic short-distance dash forward that briefly enables Big Easy Impact when using Bayou Bash immediately after the dash. *Big Easy Impact consumes a charge from Bayou Bash and causes Gambit to dash a longer distance, creating three small explosions that damage enemies and heal allies as he travels. **This proves to be a solid mobility tool when backing up or escaping from fights, since it also has two charges. Healing Hearts consumes a stack of Sleight of Hand to grant healing-over-time to self for 6 seconds, and enable either Bridge Boost [Flush] or Purifying Pick-Up [Raise] at the cost of another stack. Bridge Boost is great for healing sustain, while Purifying Pick-Up is an extremely powerful counter to stuns and debuffs. *Bridge Boost launches a targeted healing projectile to an ally that bounces onto other nearby allies up to six times. It also amplifies healing received for 4 seconds. *Purifying Pick-Up throws a swarm of projectiles all around Gambit that heal allies and cleanse negative effects upon impact. Allies can be purified up to two times per cast, while Gambit can only be affected once. Breaking Spades consumes a stack of Sleight of Hand to grant a damage boost to self for 6 seconds, and enable either Explosive Trick [Flush] or Bidding Barrage [Raise] at the cost of another stack. Explosive Trick can be used for its debuffs, while Bidding Barrage is great for driving back enemies. *Explosive Trick launches an explosive projectile that inflicts healing reduction for 4 seconds upon impact. *Bidding Barrage throws a spread of a total of ten explosive projectiles forward that also knock back enemies. Activating either a Flush Empowerment or Raise Empowerment ability will also reload all ammo for Kinetic Cards, allowing you to cycle between your rounds for increased efficiency in healing or damage output. His Ultimate, Ragin' Royal Flush, can be activated to target Gambit himself and allies within line of sight to summon aces at their position, providing an instant burst of healing and cleansing negative effects upon activation. While the aces are active, targets gain healing-over-time, 50% increased movement speed, increased jump height, deal additional bursts of 20% bonus damage to enemies upon hit, and allies charge their Ultimate 30% faster. *The burst damage effect can be triggered through any ability and has a short interval between activations when landing a hit. It automatically deals damage to the hit target no matter what, proving it much stronger in combination with lingering area-of-effect abilities and high firing speed attacks. Weaknesses: High skill ceiling to manage cooldowns and resources. * Each stack of Sleight of Hand has a 8 seconds cooldown to regenerate, and cannot be gained through any other methods besides passive recharge. * Burning through Sleight of Hand charges forces the player to rely on their weakest form of healing and damage output. Kinetic Cards cannot deal critical hits. Cajun Charge has a very long cooldown that prevents Big Easy Impact from being effectively cycled; and while Bayou Bash has a short cooldown, the ability on its own has a very short range and does not provide enough sustain to always use it outside of the dash.

strategyGuide.sourceTitle: Gambit strategy on Marvel Rivals Wiki
strategyGuide.summary: Gambit plays as a fight stabilizer who dives or pressures backline angles and stabilizes teammates through burst pressure.
paragraphs: Gambit is a Strategist hero who frequently lives up to his namesake, offering an Ace for every situation with his limited but indisputably life-saving Deck Empowerments. When it comes to powerful bursts of utility Gambit quite literally holds all of the cards. With his ability to queue up two separate Deck buffs Gambit can improvise on the fly, his role fluctuating between a traditional healing Strategist and a dive Duelist. All of which topped off with a nuclear ultimate that can completely turn the tide of a match. If quickly placing bets to steal the advantage in short term scuffles appeals to you, Gambit is the man for the job.

overview: Strengths: Mid-range bruiser with powerful area-of-denial abilities, capable of creating obstacles to protect his allies or block enemy fire. Has slightly higher health compared to other Vanguards. His primary fire, Vine Strike, extends Groot's arms to deal damage from a long range. Despite its appearance, it counts as a projectile. His secondary fire, Spore Bomb, launches a projectile which splits itself into six smaller shards upon impact. Can deal very high damage if both the main projectile and its splinters hit the target. His melee attack, Furious Flora, is distinct for dealing knockback every two hits. It can be weaved with his Vine Strike for higher damage-per-second. Groot is able to build wooden walls that can block enemy view; players can change the direction the walls are built to even serve as platforms. These interact directly with his passive, Flora Colossus, which grants Groot the ability to "awaken" the wooden walls if he's nearby them, which provides extra effects. * Ironwood Walls have higher health (700) and are longer in width. When awakened they provide Groot with 40 bonus health-per-second (up to 250). Groot can only have one active at a time. * Thornlash Walls have much lower health (250) and are shorter in width. When awakened they deal damage to enemies nearby that are attacked by Groot and his team. Groot can have up to three active at a time, and can build them continuously by holding the button. ** Throwing Spore Bomb directly at the wall if there is an enemy near it will cause the wall to lash out multiple times, both to the bomb as well to the split spores that spawn from it. * Both walls can deny plenty of attacks and even Ultimates. In particular, it can block Jeff the Land Shark's It's Jeff! if the wall is placed in front of him before he tries to devour the team, as it'll block his line of sight. * Placing any of Groot's walls over certain hero-spawned obstacles will instantly destroy them, such as Peni Parker's Arachno Mines, Moon Knight's Ancient Ankhs, and Namor's Aquatic Dominions. His Ultimate, Strangling Prison, launches vines that pull enemies in and imprison them for a few seconds while dealing damage-over-time. Enemies affected by this ability cannot move but are still able to attack and use non-mobility abilities. * If Groot throws the Ultimate at one of his walls, enemies will be pulled and pinned onto it. Weaknesses: Requires proper skill to play effectively as the wooden walls can be detrimental to his own team since they can block his allies' own abilities. Not very effective in moving objectives since he is forced to constantly reposition his wooden walls and burn his cooldowns. * Moving objectives will instantly destroy his walls if they are placed in front of them. Is vulnerable if both his wooden walls are on cooldown and destroyed. No mobility tools, combined with his rather slow speed and tall and large frame make him easy to hit. Lower attack range compared to other ranged heroes.

strategyGuide.sourceTitle: Groot strategy on Marvel Rivals Wiki
strategyGuide.summary: Groot plays as a space-maker who dives or pressures backline angles and protects space or peels pressure. Key tools: Thornlash Wall, Ironwood Wall.
paragraphs: Groot has a main ability that is unique to his character, which is being able to place walls. By placing Groots Ironwood Walls or Thornlash Walls, Groot can effectively seperate the frontline of the enemy team from the backline. By doing this, Groot's walls block incoming healing, allowing your team to swiftly eliminate the seperated tanks.
